what_it_does: Removes the broad using(true) placeholder SELECT policies that earlier migrations (0003/0004) shipped on channels, channel_posts, dna_tokens, design_seeds and design_submissions, revokes anon SELECT on the shared tables, and runs a DO-block assertion that fails the migration if any of those broad policies still exist.
observations: Header labels this C-1, an adversarial-review CRITICAL fix, explaining that Postgres ORs all permissive policies so a broad using(true) widens past the scoped 0005/0006 policies.
Drops named broad read policies for both authenticated and anon on channels and channel_posts (0003) and on the three collab tables (0004).
Revokes SELECT from anon on channels, channel_posts, dna_tokens, design_seeds, design_submissions.
Sanity DO block queries pg_policies for the listed policy names with qual='true' and raises an exception if any remain.
Header notes 0003/0004 SOURCE were also fixed; this migration is belt-and-suspenders for already-applied environments and fresh db reset.
standouts: The post-condition assertion (raise exception if a broad using(true) policy survives) — the migration verifies its own effect rather than assuming it.
The stated Postgres semantics that permissive policies are OR-combined, which is the actual root cause of the leak.
conventions: Self-verifying migration via a DO $$ ... $$ guard block.
Detailed header tying the change to an adversarial review finding (C-1).
notes: This is the security hardening that deletes the permissive placeholder read policies so the scoped boundary/design_client policies become authoritative, and asserts it stuck.
questions: Do the 0005/0006 scoped policies fully cover every legitimate read that the dropped broad policies previously allowed (i.e. no functionality regression)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: header states it drops broad read policies and revokes anon SELECT so scoped policies win, and the SQL plus the assertion do exactly that.
apparent_themes: Defense for already-applied and fresh-reset environments
intention_signals: Trying to close a real read-leak where placeholder permissive policies OR-widened past the intended scoped policies, and to guarantee the fix cannot silently fail.
novelty: Embedding a fail-loud assertion that the broad policies are truly gone, turning the migration into its own verification.
contribution: Contributes the corrective removal of OR-widening broad read policies plus an in-migration assertion guard that the scoped policy regime is authoritative.
summary_for_embedding: Supabase migration that drops broad using(true) SELECT policies and anon SELECT grants on the shared design tables and asserts none remain, so scoped RLS policies are authoritative.
intention_for_embedding: To fix a critical read-leak where permissive placeholder policies bypassed scoped access control, and to verify the fix held across applied and fresh environments.
